What is the name of the device that changes an electrical current to a magnetic field? The device that changes an electrical current to a magnetic field is called an electric motor. Electric motors are used to transform electrical energy into mechanical energy. They work by utilizing the interaction between a magnetic field and an electric current to produce motion. In an electric motor, the magnetic field is created by permanent magnets or electromagnets, and the electric current flows through a coil of wire. As the current flows through the coil, it generates a magnetic field, which interacts with the magnetic field of the permanent magnets or electromagnets, causing the coil to rotate. This rotation is then converted into mechanical energy, allowing the motor to perform various tasks such as running appliances, powering vehicles, or driving machinery.
